items.q: What is the 'Fragestellung' in a research project?
items.correct: A focused research question the project answers
items.why: Students treat the project as descriptive; a good Fragestellung poses an arguable question.

items.q: Why reference sources in the project?
items.correct: To credit them and let the work be verified
items.misconception: Because longer bibliographies always score higher
items.why: Students under-reference; a clear Literaturverzeichnis shows research breadth and avoids plagiarism.

items.q: How is the research project assessed at A-level?
items.why: Students expect a written paper; the project is defended aloud in German.

items.q: Why use German-language sources for the project?
items.correct: To research authentically and develop language skills
items.misconception: Because English sources are forbidden everywhere
items.why: It's To research authentically and develop language skills. “Because English sources are forbidden everywhere” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What does critical thinking ('kritisches Denken') involve here?
items.why: Correct answer: Comparing and evaluating different viewpoints. “Copying one website exactly” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How is the research project assessed?
items.why: It's Orally, in the speaking exam. “In a listening test only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
